exposure_rationale: The occupation of Other Instructors involves a blend of knowledge work and interpersonal interaction, particularly in education and community services. While AI can assist in administrative tasks and provide educational resources, the core functions of teaching, mentoring, and social work require human presence and judgment, limiting the overall cognitive AI exposure.

title: Sheriffs and bailiffs
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
A college diploma or bachelor's degree in legal studies is usually required.
Sheriffs require experience as a deputy sheriff or bailiff and/or in custodial/escort or security work.
exposure_rationale: Sheriffs and bailiffs primarily engage in physical and interpersonal work, such as enforcing court orders and managing legal processes, which limits their exposure to cognitive AI. While AI may assist with administrative tasks like scheduling or documentation, the core responsibilities require human judgment and presence, resulting in a low to moderate AI exposure score.

title: Correctional service officers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
Post-secondary education in correctional services, police studies or criminology is required in New Brunswick, Quebec, and Alberta and Prince Edward Island.
Correctional officer recruits must successfully complete the Correctional Service of Canada training course to be employed by federal institutions.

title: By-law enforcement and other regulatory officers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a college program or courses in law and security or other related field or experience in a related administrative or regulatory occupation is usually required.

Zoning inspectors or zoning enforcement officers may require a college diploma as a civil engineering or architectural technologist or technician and several years of experience in enforcement and/or building construction or a related field.
exposure_rationale: By-law enforcement and regulatory officers engage in a mix of knowledge work and interpersonal interactions, such as investigating complaints, enforcing regulations, and communicating with the public. While AI can assist with data analysis and documentation, the role requires significant human judgment and presence, limiting the overall cognitive AI exposure.

title: Border services, customs, and immigration officers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.
A four-week online training and a four-and-half-month specialized training at the CBSA College are required for border services officers.

Successful pass of the officer trainee entrance exam (OTEE), successful completion of the Canadian Firearms Safety Course (CGSC) and the Canadian Restricted Firearms Safety Course (CRFSC) is required for border services officers.
exposure_rationale: Border services, customs, and immigration officers primarily engage in interpersonal work that involves assessing individuals and handling complex situations that require human judgment and discretion. While AI may assist in data processing and administrative tasks, the core responsibilities of this occupation necessitate a physical presence and human interaction, limiting the overall cognitive AI exposure.

title: Operations Members of the Canadian Armed Forces
exposure_rationale: Operations Members of the Canadian Armed Forces primarily engage in physical and interpersonal tasks that require a human presence, such as training, coordination, and direct engagement with personnel. While some administrative tasks may be assisted by AI, the core responsibilities of this occupation remain largely hands-on and are not fundamentally digital, limiting overall AI exposure.

title: Home child care providers
education_req: Completion of secondary school may be required.
Home child care providers, parent's helpers and foster parents may require completion of a training program in child care or a related field.
Child care or household management experience may be required.
exposure_rationale: Home child care providers primarily engage in physical and interpersonal work, focusing on the care and development of children, which limits the impact of cognitive AI. While AI may assist with administrative tasks or scheduling, the core responsibilities of nurturing and supervising children require human presence and emotional intelligence, resulting in low AI exposure.

title: Home support workers, caregivers and related occupations
education_req: Some secondary school education is usually required.
Home management experience may be required.
College or other courses in home support may be required.
exposure_rationale: Home support workers and caregivers primarily engage in interpersonal work that involves direct human interaction, emotional support, and physical assistance, which limits the impact of cognitive AI. While AI may assist with scheduling or minor administrative tasks, the core responsibilities of providing care and support cannot be easily automated or replaced by AI technologies.

title: Primary combat members of the Canadian Armed Forces
exposure_rationale: The primary combat members of the Canadian Armed Forces engage in predominantly physical and interpersonal work, which involves direct human presence and decision-making in dynamic environments. title: Student monitors, crossing guards and related occupations
education_req: Completion of secondary school may be required.
First aid certification and cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) training may be required.
exposure_rationale: The role of student monitors and crossing guards primarily involves physical presence and interpersonal interaction, focusing on safety and supervision rather than cognitive tasks. While AI may assist in scheduling or communication, the core responsibilities are hands-on and cannot be easily replaced or augmented by cognitive AI.

title: Library, archive, museum and art gallery managers
education_req: Library managers

Archive managers

title: Managers - publishing, motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts
education_req: A university degree or college diploma in communications, broadcasting, journalism or other arts discipline is usually required.

title: Recreation, sports and fitness program and service directors

title: Librarians

For telecommunication line and cable installers and repairers, completion of a four-year telecommunications line and cable apprenticeship program or a combination of over three years work experience in the trade and some industry- related or other specialized courses or completion of a two-year college program in electronics is usually required.

For cable television maintenance technicians, completion of a college program in electronics or a combination of college, correspondence or industry courses related to electronics and electrical systems and on-the-job training or completion of a four-year cable television technician apprenticeship program is required.
exposure_rationale: The role of telecommunications line and cable installers and repairers primarily involves physical tasks such as installation and maintenance of cables, which require hands-on skills and physical presence. While some aspects of their work may involve digital tools for scheduling or diagnostics, the core tasks remain largely manual and interpersonal, limiting the impact of cognitive AI.

title: Telecommunications equipment installation and cable television service technicians
education_req: Completion of secondary school is required.

Telephone and switch network installers and repairers require completion of an apprenticeship program ranging from three to four years or a combination of over three years work experience in the trade and some high school, college or industry related courses.

Cable television service technicians require completion of a college program in electronics or a combination of college, correspondence or industry courses related to electronics and electrical systems and on-the-job training or completion of a four-year cable television technician apprenticeship program.
exposure_rationale: The role of telecommunications equipment installation and cable television service technicians involves significant physical and hands-on work, such as installing and maintaining equipment on-site. While there may be some administrative tasks that could be assisted by AI, the core responsibilities require physical presence and manual skills, limiting the overall cognitive AI exposure.

title: Plumbers
education_req: Completion of secondary school is usually required.

Completion of a four- to five-year apprenticeship program or a combination of over five years of work experience in the trade and some high school, college or industry courses in plumbing is usually required to be eligible for trade certification.

Trade certification is compulsory in Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, New Brunswick, Quebec, Ontario, Saskatchewan and Alberta and available, but voluntary, in Newfoundland and Labrador, Manitoba, British Columbia, the Yukon, the Northwest Territories and Nunavut.
exposure_rationale: Plumbers primarily engage in physical, hands-on work that requires on-site presence to install and repair plumbing systems. While some administrative tasks may be assisted by AI, the core responsibilities of plumbing involve manual skills and real-time problem-solving that AI cannot easily replicate.
